flags.concern: Gallery selection scope: mounting the new selection provider in VideoEditorShell or the video-editor context tree will not make it available to GenerationsPane, because the pane is mounted from app layout outside both full and compact VideoEditorProvider trees.
flags.evidence: src/app/Layout.tsx:128-131 renders <GenerationsPane /> at layout scope. The full editor wraps VideoEditorShell in VideoEditorProvider only in src/tools/video-editor/pages/VideoEditorPage.tsx:293-299, and the compact editor does the same in src/features/editor/components/EditorPaneTab.tsx:152-155. GenerationsPane itself renders GenerationsPaneGallery inside that separate tree in src/features/gallery/components/GenerationsPane/GenerationsPane.tsx:153-176.

flags.concern: Timeline-agent attachment semantics: feeding gallery selections into the existing selected_clips pipeline unchanged is not sufficient, because the edge function interprets clip_id as a timeline clip id for prompt context and shot/generation resolution.
flags.evidence: src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAgentSession.ts:266-275 sends each attachment as { clip_id, url, media_type }. On the backend, supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/prompts.ts:11-18 looks for id=${clip.clip_id} in the timeline summary, and supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/clips.ts:4-14 resolves generation ids by config.clips.find((clip) => clip.id === clip_id). supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/create-task.ts:62-68 depends on that resolution to reuse/create the right shot. Gallery generation ids will not satisfy those lookups.

flags.concern: Gallery multi-select interactions: the plan drops explicit requirements for Shift-toggle, lasso/marquee selection, and dragging all selected gallery items onto the timeline together; those behaviors do not already exist in the gallery code.
flags.evidence: Current gallery click handling is single-item only: src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem/hooks/useItemInteraction.ts:52-54 just calls onImageClick(image). Drag/drop is also single-item only: src/shared/lib/dnd/dragDrop.ts:84-90 defines a single GenerationDropData, src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem.tsx:271-278 serializes one generation on drag start, and src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts:346-351 consumes a single generation drop. Without explicit work here, the user-requested lasso and multi-item drag-to-timeline flow will remain unsupported.
